location: 15 Galloway Street, Dumfries, DG2 7TL
why_them: No website whatsoever — presence is Facebook-only despite being a well-established, highly-rated Dumfries clinic with over 23 years of experience. No online booking, no service or pricing information available digitally. An experienced multi-treatment clinic (acupuncture, joint manipulation, corticosteroid injections) is losing new patients every day to competitors with functional websites.
recommended_service: New website build with online booking (Cliniko or similar), service/pricing pages, and Google Maps integration

location: Queen of the South Arena, Dumfries, DG2 9BA
why_them: Website returns 404 errors on key pages — the site is effectively broken and unreachable for potential patients. Clinic has been established for 31 years with two branches, meaning there's established revenue to justify investment. No online booking and relies on a mobile number only.
recommended_service: Full website rebuild with online booking, service pages for all treatment types, and a functioning contact/email system

location: Chambers House, High Street, Moffat, DG10 9ED
why_them: Outdated site using legacy jQuery and early 2010s design — a multi-discipline centre offering osteopathy, acupuncture, counselling, hypnotherapy, and more deserves a site that reflects its breadth. No pricing visible, the booking link goes to an external system that feels disconnected, and the old design fails to convey the quality of a well-established Moffat practice.
recommended_service: Full site redesign integrating all therapy disciplines with individual service pages, integrated booking, therapist profiles, and transparent pricing

location: Lochvale House, Georgetown, Dumfries (also Dalbeattie)
why_them: A multi-practitioner osteopathic clinic with five named osteopaths and two clinic sites — significant revenue and prestige — yet the website is basic WordPress with no online booking, no pricing, and limited service detail. A practice of this size and quality in Dumfries town centre deserves a site that converts visitors into patients.
recommended_service: Professional multi-practitioner website with practitioner profiles, service/pricing pages, online booking, and patient information resources

location: Leslie House, 50 St Cuthbert's Street, Kirkcudbright, DG6 4DZ
why_them: Outdated early-2010s website design for a practice that offers premium cosmetic dentistry, dental implants, and facial aesthetics — a significant mismatch between service quality and digital first impression. No online booking and the fee guide is buried in navigation. Kirkcudbright's tourist footfall means they have out-of-area patients who will judge by website quality.
recommended_service: Modern website redesign with cosmetic dentistry showcase, online booking, prominent fee guide, and before/after gallery

location: Galloway House, The Crichton, Bankend Road, Dumfries, DG1 4ZZ
why_them: Professional psychology practice using a Gmail address for all enquiries — undermines trust and credibility for a clinical psychology service. No phone number visible on the site, no online booking, and no pricing. Multi-service practice offering medico-legal work, CBT, and EMDR represents solid revenue potential.
recommended_service: Professional website with branded email, structured service and fee pages, a discreet self-referral contact form, and GDPR-compliant intake process

location: King Street Clinic, Dumfries, DG2 9AL
why_them: Outdated website with a COVID-19 notice from March 2022 still displayed — clearly not updated in years. No email contact, no online booking, and patients must 'call and leave a message' to book. Has pricing on site but no modern booking capability, which is now standard for solo private practitioners.
recommended_service: Website refresh/redesign with online booking (e.g. Fresha or Square Appointments) and email contact form

location: Dumfries, DG
why_them: Only a Wix subdomain site (wixsite.com/website) with no custom domain — signals a very DIY, low-investment digital presence. No online booking and no visible service or pricing information. A home-visiting podiatrist with no professional web presence is invisible to patients searching Google.
recommended_service: Professional website with custom domain, service pages, pricing, and an online booking or enquiry form
source: Google search, Bark.com, direct site review

location: 34 Bridge Street, Stranraer, DG9 7JF
why_them: Multi-site dental practice (Stranraer and Newton Stewart) with an outdated website design and no online booking — visitors are directed to call rather than book online. No email contact visible and the Newton Stewart location lacks its own contact details on the site.
recommended_service: Full website redesign with online booking, individual location pages for Stranraer and Newton Stewart, treatment and fee guides

location: Galloway House, The Crichton, Dumfries, DG1 4ZZ
why_them: Site has an expired SSL certificate — browsers show security warnings to visitors. Specialist neurological physiotherapy (Parkinson's, stroke rehab) is a premium niche, yet the digital presence actively undermines confidence. Uses a Gmail address for contact.
recommended_service: New secure (HTTPS) website with professional email, specialist service pages for neurological conditions, patient testimonials, and home-visit booking capability

location: 5/7 Nith Place, Dumfries, DG1 2PL
why_them: Long-established independent Dumfries dental practice on Squarespace with a dated aesthetic, no online booking, and no email contact — only a phone and emergency mobile. Given the full range of cosmetic and restorative services offered (implants, Invisalign, whitening), the lack of a booking system represents a clear competitive disadvantage.
recommended_service: Website redesign or Squarespace upgrade with online booking, treatment gallery, patient registration flow, and email contact

location: Suite 1 Galloway House, The Crichton, Dumfries, DG1 4ZZ
why_them: Wix-built site for a specialist Five Element acupuncture and Chinese medicine clinic with no visible online booking and contact via a personal Gmail address. Specialising in chronic pain, fertility, and mental health — premium niches requiring professional credibility — yet the Wix site and Gmail contact do not inspire confidence.
recommended_service: Professional website with specialty landing pages (fertility, pain, mental health), patient FAQ, a booking integration, and branded email
source: Google search, Yell, direct site review

location: High Street, Moniaive, Thornhill, DG3 4HN
why_them: Wix-built site with no visible online booking, no email address, and no pricing information — the only contact shown is a phone number. For a private therapy practice offering CBT and trauma-focused treatment, a site that fails to inspire digital confidence will cost them referrals.
recommended_service: New professional website with a confidential contact/self-referral form, service descriptions for each therapy type, FAQ section, and online booking

location: 9 Irving Street, Dumfries, DG1 1EL
why_them: Independent family-run opticians in central Dumfries with a website that uses outdated jQuery, has no online booking, no pricing, and no email contact — only a phone number. As the main independent alternative to Specsavers in Dumfries, a stronger digital presence would help them compete against the national chain.
recommended_service: Website redesign with online appointment booking, eye test/service descriptions, frame gallery, and email contact

location: 32A High Street, Gatehouse of Fleet, DG7 2HA
why_them: Outdated website design for a specialist independent audiologist covering a wide rural D&G area (Castle Douglas, Kirkcudbright, Newton Stewart, Stranraer, Wigtown). No online booking, minimal service detail, and no clear pricing — yet patients across the region are actively searching for hearing specialists online.
recommended_service: New modern website with service pages for hearing tests, wax removal, and hearing aids, online booking or callback form, and coverage area map

location: Crossmichael Complementary Therapy Centre, Crossmichael, DG7 3AP
why_them: Site has no email contact, no online booking, and directs all enquiries to a phone number only — a major barrier for clients who prefer discrete digital contact when reaching out about mental health. The site is built on a template builder and lacks a modern, trust-building design appropriate for a counselling service.
recommended_service: Redesign with secure contact form, online booking integration, testimonials section, and a reassuring brand aesthetic suited to mental health
source: Google search, Counselling Directory, direct site review

location: Lochthorn Medical Centre, Edinburgh Road, Heathhall, Dumfries
why_them: Two-practitioner acupuncture clinic with a basic Astra/WordPress site that has no pricing, no online booking despite offering a 'free 10-minute consultation', and a visually static design. The consultation offer is excellent for conversions but the site provides no way to book it digitally — every interested patient must make a phone call, creating friction and likely lost enquiries.
recommended_service: Website upgrade with integrated free consultation booking form, pricing page, expanded treatment descriptions, and practitioner bios

location: The Print Room, 8 New Road, Wigtown, DG8 9HN
why_them: Has online booking via Cliniko (a positive) but the site is basic WordPress with no email contact, no pricing, and a design that won't inspire confidence. Being the only physio practice in Wigtown — a book-town tourist area — means there's real upside to a stronger web presence that also targets the visitor market.
recommended_service: Website upgrade with improved design, pricing transparency, email contact, and SEO optimisation targeting Wigtown and Newton Stewart searches

location: Saint Mary's Street, Sanquhar, DG4 6BW
why_them: Wix site with no visible pricing, no email, and no online booking — the phone number is the only route to book, a significant barrier for clients seeking counselling for sensitive issues. Sanquhar is an under-served D&G town; a better digital presence would help this practitioner stand out in a sparse local market.
recommended_service: Improved website with a reassuring design, confidential contact form, session information/FAQ page, and online appointment booking

location: 13 New Market Street, Castle Douglas, DG7
why_them: Wix-built site for a sports and remedial massage therapist based in Castle Douglas with no visible pricing, no service detail, and no clear online booking functionality. Castle Douglas and the wider Glenkens area have limited competition in this niche, so a practitioner with a strong digital presence would dominate local search.
recommended_service: Improved website with treatment menu, pricing, integrated online booking (e.g. Fresha), and a mobile-first design — sports massage clients skew younger and expect to book online
